# SOW Playbook Matrix

Reference table from the Trinetix Contract Playbook. Use these as the deterministic clause-by-clause ceiling alongside the per-type SKILL.md.

| ID / Topic | Preferred position | Acceptable fallback | Red flags | Escalation | AI instruction |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SOW-001 Mandatory fields | Each SOW must include project start date, expiration date, type of engagement, billing model, service description, rate card, contacts, term, and project details. | Use “TBD” only if not material before signing and owner is named. | Blank fields, inconsistent dates, no MSA date, wrong client legal name, wrong Vendor entity. | Legal counsel before signature. | Check all placeholders and comment colors before release. |
| SOW-002 Engagement type | Use Dedicated Team / Project-based / Capacity-based. Match legal protections to model. | Hybrid model allowed if rate card and obligations are clear. | Fixed-price wording hidden in T&M SOW; dedicated team treated as managed outcome. | Delivery/Finance review. | Identify engagement type and test clauses for consistency. |
| SOW-003 Scope of services | Services are advisory/consulting IT services delivered by consultants under client requirements and instructions. | Add project-specific details in project description and appendix. | Hard obligation to achieve business result; undefined “complete solution”; responsibility for client operations. | Delivery/CLCO review. | Flag outcome guarantees and vague deliverables. |
| SOW-004 Additional services | Additional services require separate agreement/appendix. | Emergency work may proceed if email approval and billing basis are clear. | Client can demand additional services at no extra cost. | Finance/Delivery review. | Check if scope creep is controlled. |
| SOW-005 Vendor obligations | Vendor monitors performance reasonably, provides instructions, addresses reasonable complaints, reports status. | More frequent reporting acceptable if practical and priced. | Strict SLA/project success guarantee not priced or defined. | Delivery review. | Separate resource-management duties from outcome commitments. |
| SOW-006 Client obligations | Client must plan, provide requirements, clarify, approve, communicate daily/as agreed, maintain task lists, share documentation, and cooperate. | Tailor obligations for client tools and processes. | Client obligations deleted; no delay relief for late feedback/access. | Delivery/Legal review. | Verify that client dependencies are explicit. |
| SOW-007 Delay relief | Vendor not responsible for deficiencies caused by client non-cooperation; deadlines extend by equivalent delay. | Accept reasonable notice obligation before extension. | Vendor remains liable for client delay; fixed deadlines despite late client inputs. | Delivery/CLCO review. | Flag missing time/cost adjustment. |
| SOW-008 Consultant availability | Regular business hours in consultant location; holidays and temporary absence rules apply; no client payment for absences. | Special hours allowed with written approval and premium rates. | 24/7 availability, named resources always available, no holiday/absence carve-out. | Delivery/Finance review. | Check special hour commitments and premium rates. |
| SOW-009 Exclusive utilization | Client gets exclusive use within committed utilization. Vendor does not assign consultant elsewhere during that committed period. | Exclusive only for paid committed allocation. | Exclusive access without committed utilization payment. | Finance/Delivery review. | Check that exclusivity aligns with payment obligation. |
| SOW-010 Fees and rate card | Fees based on rates, role, seniority, committed utilization. Total cost cap may be included if used intentionally. | Caps allowed only if they do not create fixed-price obligations unless approved. | Total cap inconsistent with committed utilization; no rate card; missing currency. | Finance review. | Extract rates, cap, committed utilization, currency, and billing cycle. |
| SOW-011 Overtime / special hours | >9 hours/day or >45 hours/week at 150%; weekends/holidays/night hours at 200%; only with client written request or approval. | Client-specific premium rules may be used if commercially accepted. | No overtime premium; mandatory unpaid extra work. | Finance/Delivery approval. | Flag premium-rate deviations. |
| SOW-012 Expenses and travel | Client reimburses pre-approved reasonable travel/accommodation and listed costs; Vendor may request advance payment. | Client travel policy may apply if reviewed and feasible. | Uncapped travel without approval; Vendor bears client-requested travel costs. | Finance review. | Check approval, reimbursement, per diem, visa/healthcare, lodging, and transport. |
| SOW-013 Acceptance / approvals | Client responds to service completion approval requests within 2 business days. For deliverables, add acceptance criteria and deemed acceptance. | Longer review period acceptable if specific and bounded. | No deemed acceptance; subjective acceptance; indefinite rejection rights. | Delivery/Legal review. | If deliverables exist, ensure objective acceptance criteria and deemed acceptance. |
| SOW-014 Contacts | Business, legal, finance, delivery, technical, and notice contacts should be complete. | Use role-based email aliases when possible. | Missing billing/contact channels; notices only via informal chat. | Legal/Finance review. | Check contacts are complete before signature. |
| SOW-015 Term and termination | SOW should align with MSA termination. Add ramp-down and committed utilization consequences when needed. | Specific project term acceptable. | Immediate convenience termination without payment for committed allocation or work done. | Finance/Delivery review. | Check if early termination preserves payment economics. |
| SOW-016 Project description appendix | Use appendix for deliverables, milestones, assumptions, out-of-scope items, tools, repositories, and acceptance. | For dedicated team, keep deliverable commitments light unless project-based. | Empty project description for project-based work; no assumptions/dependencies. | Delivery review. | Flag missing project details based on engagement type. |
